subject: Science
chapter: The Invisible Living World: Beyond Our Naked Eye
source_pdf: 2.The Invisible Living World Beyond Our Naked Eye.pdf

key_points: The microscope enabled the discovery of the microscopic world and led to the development of microbiology
Cells are the structural and functional units of all living organisms
Cell specialization allows multicellular organisms to perform complex biological functions
Microorganisms contribute significantly to nutrient cycles and ecosystem balance
Microbial processes such as fermentation and nitrogen fixation support food production and agriculture
examples: Nerve cells transmit signals throughout the body
Yeast fermentation produces carbon dioxide during bread making
Lactobacillus bacteria produce lactic acid during curd formation
Rhizobium bacteria enrich soil fertility in leguminous plants
Microalgae contribute significantly to global oxygen production
processes: Fermentation: Microorganisms convert sugars into energy, gases, or acids in anaerobic conditions
Nitrogen fixation: Nitrogen gas from the atmosphere is converted into ammonia or nitrates usable by plants
Decomposition: Microorganisms break down complex organic matter into simpler nutrients
Photosynthesis in microalgae: Light energy is used to convert carbon dioxide and water into food and oxygen
exam_tips: Explain the relationship between cell structure and cell function
Understand how microbial processes support ecosystems and agriculture
Study real-life applications of microorganisms such as fermentation and composting
Be able to analyze both beneficial and harmful roles of microorganisms
